title: EDEN experiment lease
description: An ExperimentLease record issued by the control plane (spec/v0/11-control-plane.md §4.2). A lease is a per-experiment, time-bounded ownership claim held by exactly one orchestrator replica at any instant; the holder is authorized to run the chapter 03 §6.2 orchestrator decisions for the leased experiment. Lease records are observed through the control-plane wire endpoints (spec/v0/07-wire-protocol.md §15.2); they are NOT part of the task-store-server's runtime experiment object (spec/v0/schemas/experiment.schema.json).

lease_id.description: Opaque identifier assigned by the control plane on acquire. Stable across renewals; replaced on a fresh acquire over an expired or released predecessor.

experiment_id.description: The experiment this lease binds. Matches the chapter 02 §1.1 identifier grammar.

holder.description: The deployment-scoped worker_id of the orchestrator replica holding the lease (spec/v0/11-control-plane.md §6). Distinct from any per-experiment worker_id.

holder_instance.description: Per-process UUID supplied by the orchestrator at acquire time (spec/v0/11-control-plane.md §4.7). Defends against duplicate worker_id misconfigurations across replicas. renew_lease and release_lease MUST present the same value; mismatch returns 409 eden://error/lease-instance-mismatch.

acquired_at.description: Wall-clock time at which the lease was acquired. Set by the control plane; not advanced on renewal.

expires_at.description: Wall-clock time after which the lease is no longer active. Set to acquired_at + lease_duration at acquire; reset to now + lease_duration on each successful renew_lease. The control plane is the source of truth — the orchestrator MUST use the control-plane-computed value, not its locally-computed now + lease_duration.

renewed_at.description: Wall-clock time of the most recent successful renew, or acquired_at if the lease has never been renewed.
